set.caption: Fig. 3.8 Glass vs. Crystal. (a) Ideal gas of N toms in a big box of size V . (b) N ideal gas atoms ach in a small box of size $V / N$ . The ideal gas on the eft has an additional entropy due to the $N ^ { \tilde { N } } / N !$ configurations of filling the different small boxes with the toms. This entropy difference is quantitatively simlar to the zero-temperature entropy difference beween a glass (with many metastable configurations) nd a crystal (with a regular lattice of atoms).
